key_insight: PINNsur extends physics-informed neural networks to PDEs on curved surfaces by learning surface normals and projecting Euclidean differential operators onto the manifold.
engineering_relevance: This offers a mesh-free alternative to FEM for geometry-processing and surface PDE problems, potentially reducing sensitivity to mesh quality and geometric discretization errors.
method: Train a neural field to approximate surface normals on the curved manifold.
Compute surface differential operators by projecting operators from R^3 onto the surface.
Use the framework on orientable surfaces of arbitrary curvature/topology and test convergence empirically.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports an empirical convergence test to study whether the PINN solution and the surface approximation converge toward the true PDE solution.
watch_item: Accuracy depends on both the learned geometry (normals/surface representation) and the PDE solution, so geometric approximation error can limit convergence.
paper_title: PINNsur: Physics-Informed Neural Networks for PDEs on Curved Surfaces
